rules.httpClientNoOutgoing: HTTP client "{label}" has no outgoing connection
rules.clientGroupNoOutgoing: Client group "{label}" has no outgoing connection
rules.loadBalancerNoBackends: Load balancer "{label}" has no connected backends
rules.edgeDanglingTarget: A connection points to a non-existent node (ID: {nodeId})
rules.edgeDanglingSource: A connection comes from a non-existent node (ID: {nodeId})
rules.edgeToHostNoPort: Connection to server "{label}" has no target port configured
rules.edgeToFirewallNoPort: Connection to firewall "{label}" has no target port
rules.firewallBlocksAll: Firewall "{label}" blocks all traffic (deny with no allowed ports)
rules.portMappingDangling: Port mapping on "{label}" references a non-existent container
rules.portMismatch: Port {port} doesn't match any port mapping on "{label}"
rules.apiGatewayDanglingRoute: Route rule "{pattern}" on "{label}" targets unconnected service "{service}"
rules.apiGatewayRulesNoEdges: API Gateway "{label}" has route rules but no outgoing connections
rules.clientGroupOverloads: Group "{clientLabel}" ({clients} clients) targets a server limited to {maxConc} connections
rules.httpServerNoResources: HTTP server "{label}" has no resource configuration
rules.circuitBreakerNoIncoming: Circuit breaker "{label}" has no incoming connections (useless)
rules.circuitBreakerNoOutgoing: Circuit breaker "{label}" has no outgoing connections (no service to protect)
rules.cacheNoFallback: Cache "{label}" has no outgoing connection (no DB fallback on cache miss)
rules.cdnNoOrigin: CDN "{label}" has no origin server connected (hit ratio: {hitRatio}%, misses will fail)
rules.loadBalancerSingleBackend: Load balancer "{label}" has only 1 backend (nothing to balance)
rules.messageQueueNoConsumers: Queue "{label}" has no connected consumers (messages never processed)
rules.hostServerMappingsNoIncoming: Server "{label}" has port mappings but no incoming traffic
rules.apiGatewayDuplicatePriority: API Gateway "{label}" has {count} rules with same priority {priority} (non-deterministic routing)
rules.apiServiceNoName: Service "{label}" has no service name (required for API Gateway routing)
rules.serverlessTimeoutLessThanColdStart: Timeout ({timeout}ms) of "{label}" is less than cold start ({coldStart}ms)
rules.serverlessZeroConcurrency: "{label}" has concurrency limit of 0 (no requests can be processed)
rules.backgroundJobCronNoSchedule: Cron job "{label}" has no schedule defined
rules.backgroundJobBatchNoSize: Batch job "{label}" has no batch size defined
rules.httpClientZeroInterval: HTTP client "{label}" is in loop mode with 0ms interval (maximum spam)
rules.highErrorRate: Server "{label}" has an error rate of {rate}% (very high)
rules.lbWeightedNoWeights: Load balancer "{label}" uses weighted algorithm (default weight = 1 for all)
rules.circuitBreakerTightTimeout: Circuit breaker "{label}" has very short timeout ({timeout}ms)
rules.containerNoParent: Container "{label}" is not hosted in a server
rules.containerWrongParent: Container "{label}" is inside "{parentLabel}" instead of a host-server
rules.portMappingNotChild: Port mapping {port} on "{label}" references a container that is not a child of this server

welcome.title: Welcome to Architecture Simulator
welcome.description: This interactive tutorial will guide you step by step to create your first distributed architecture and run a simulation. Follow each step to discover the main features.

componentsPanel.title: Components Panel
componentsPanel.description: This panel (RACK) contains 20 components organized in 7 categories: Simulation, Infrastructure, Data, Resilience, Compute, Cloud and Zones. Drag and drop a component onto the canvas to add it to your architecture.

headerTools.title: Toolbar
headerTools.description:
**TPL** — load a predefined architecture
**YAML** — define architecture in YAML code
**VALID** — validate architecture consistency
**Trash** — delete all components from the canvas

flowCanvasIntro.title: Workspace Canvas
flowCanvasIntro.description: This is where you build your architecture. Drag components from the RACK, connect them together, and organize your diagram. You can zoom, pan the view, and select components to configure them.

dragHttpClient.title: Add an HTTP Client
dragHttpClient.description: Drag the "HTTP Client" component from the left panel and drop it onto the canvas.

dragHttpServer.title: Add an HTTP Server
dragHttpServer.description: Now drag the "HTTP Server" component and drop it onto the canvas, to the right of the client.

connectNodes.title: Connect the Components
connectNodes.description: Click and hold the connection point (●) on the right side of the HTTP Client, then drag to the input point (●) on the left side of the HTTP Server to create a connection.

architectureReady.title: Architecture Ready!
architectureReady.description: Well done! Your first architecture is created. Tip: click on a component to edit its properties in the right panel (HTTP method, port, response delay, etc.).

selectHttpClient.title: Select the HTTP Client
selectHttpClient.description: Click on the "HTTP Client" component on the canvas to select it and open the properties panel.

propertiesPanelIntro.title: Properties Panel
propertiesPanelIntro.description: This panel shows the properties of the selected component. You can modify the HTTP method, path, request mode, interval, and many other parameters.

setLoopMode.title: Enable Continuous Loop
setLoopMode.description: Change the request mode from "Single Request" to "Continuous Loop". This will allow the client to send requests continuously during the simulation.

switchSimMode.title: Switch to Simulation Mode
switchSimMode.description: Click "SIM" to switch to simulation mode. In simulation mode, the architecture is locked and you can run load tests.

simControlsIntro.title: Simulation Controls
simControlsIntro.description:
**START** — launch the simulation
**PAUSE** — pause it
**STOP** — stop and generate the report
**RST** — reset
**DUR** — configure the maximum duration

startSimulation.title: Start the Simulation
startSimulation.description: Click START to launch the simulation. The HTTP client will start sending requests to the server.

observeParticles.title: Simulation Running
observeParticles.description: Watch the animated particles flowing between components. They represent requests in transit. Nodes change color based on their state (processing, success, error).

pauseSimulation.title: Pause the Simulation
pauseSimulation.description: Click PAUSE to suspend the simulation. This will let you explore the collected metrics at your own pace.

metricsTab.title: Metrics Tab
metricsTab.description: The Metrics tab displays real-time performance metrics: latency (min/avg/max), success rate, requests per second (RPS), and server resource utilization with sparklines.

outputTab.title: Output Tab
outputTab.description: The Output tab displays a real-time request log: each request sent and its response with status code, latency, and the path taken.

validationTab.title: Validation Tab
validationTab.description: The Valid tab shows architecture validation results: blocking errors (disconnected components, invalid configurations) and warnings.

tracesTab.title: Traces Tab
tracesTab.description: The Traces tab displays a waterfall view of request chains. You can visualize the complete path of a request through components with detailed timings.

stopSimulation.title: Stop the Simulation
stopSimulation.description: Click STOP to stop the simulation. A detailed report will be automatically generated with all collected metrics.

viewReport.title: Simulation Report
viewReport.description: This report summarizes your architecture's performance: total duration, success rate, average latency, active client count, and server resource saturation. Use it to identify bottlenecks.
